description:
You are a security engineer who has just rotated the API credentials for an internal
    tool.  
    A new bearer-token has already been written to  
    /home/user/credential_rotation/new_token.txt  

    A local mock API response, representing the “rotate-credentials” endpoint, is stored
    at  
    /home/user/credential_rotation/fake_endpoint.json  

    Your goal is to perform a *single* curl request that proves the new credential is
    in active use and to capture a verification log.

    Required actions (high-level):
    1. Read the exact token from /home/user/credential_rotation/new_token.txt.
    2. Issue ONE curl command that
       • targets the local file /home/user/credential_rotation/fake_endpoint.json  
         (use the file:// URL scheme so no external network is needed), and  
       • sends the header  Authorization: Bearer <token>.
    3. Capture the following details in a log file located at  
       /home/user/credential_rotation/rotation_check.log

    rotation_check.log MUST contain exactly two lines, in the order shown below,
    with no leading/trailing spaces and followed by a single newline at the end of
    each line:

        HEADER_AUTHORIZATION=Bearer <token>
        BODY_RESULT=<value_of_result_field_in_JSON>

    • <token>     – must be the literal token string read from
                    new_token.txt (do NOT hard-code it).  
    • <value_of_result_field_in_JSON> – the value of the “result” key that appears in
                    fake_endpoint.json.

    Example (illustrative – your actual token differs):

        HEADER_AUTHORIZATION=Bearer MY-NEW-TOKEN
        BODY_RESULT=success

    The automated test will:
      • check that /home/user/credential_rotation/rotation_check.log exists,  
      • verify the file has exactly two non-empty lines in the format shown,  
      • confirm the Authorization header matches the new token, and  
      • confirm the body result matches the JSON content of fake_endpoint.json.

    Perform all work as an unprivileged user; no sudo is necessary.  
    Remember: use only one curl command, and ensure the log file’s format is
    identical to the specification so the test can parse it.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (pre-populated):

    Directory: /home/user/credential_rotation
        ├── new_token.txt           – plain-text file containing exactly:
        │      SECURE-TOKEN-998877\n
        └── fake_endpoint.json      – plain-text file containing exactly:
               {
                 "result": "success"
               }\n

    Expected final state for automated verification:

    File: /home/user/credential_rotation/rotation_check.log
    Exact content (two lines, each ending with \n):

        HEADER_AUTHORIZATION=Bearer SECURE-TOKEN-998877\n
        BODY_RESULT=success\n

    The agent must not modify new_token.txt or fake_endpoint.json.
